title: Contract-publication tooling: derive client supported-versions from the canonical schema, drift regression, contract.py docstring fix
objective: Consolidated contract-tooling and documentation-hygiene task (owner carry-forward 2026-07-17): (1) generate or derive the web client's supported-contract-version runtime list (apps/web/src/lib/contract.ts SUPPORTED_CONTRACT_VERSIONS) from the canonical schema enum or the canonical generated contract artifact, eliminating the possibility that the schema publishes a new contract version while the client's manually maintained runtime list silently omits it; (2) add a negative regression test proving that publication drift or omission IS detected (a version present in the schema but absent from the client list must turn CI red); (3) correct the stale module-level description in services/api/app/profile/contract.py (lines ~16-20) which still describes 1.2.0 as the current builder version even though the builder now emits 1.3.0 (M2-T006 G3 LOW D1). AMENDED (owner directive 2026-07-20): BOTH the backend and the client supported-version declarations must be generated from the canonical schema source and protected by drift tests. The backend side already derives at import time (services/api/app/profile/contract.py _supported_versions() reads the bundled schema enum) and CI enforces bundled-copy byte-identity against packages/contracts (services/api/scripts/sync_contract_schemas.py --check); this task must PROVE and PRESERVE that chain (assert it in the drift-test evidence, do not rebuild it) while ADDING the client-side derivation + drift detection. This task must land before ANY contract 1.4.0 publication (M2-T012 depends on it). CONSTRAINTS: do NOT publish contract 1.4.0 as part of this task; do NOT change existing contract semantics merely for cleanup; preserve compatibility with 1.0.0, 1.1.0, 1.2.0, and 1.3.0. OWNER PRECONDITION: this task must be accepted before ANY contract version after 1.3.0 is published. It does not block M2-T007/T008/T009 while those fit truthfully within contract 1.3.0.
business_reason: M2-T006 G3 R-NEW-1 + owner directive 2026-07-17: every version publication is currently a coordinated atomic schema+backend+client change because the client pins a closed manual list; this task removes the silent-omission failure mode before any 1.4.0
inputs: packages/contracts/schemas/v1/property_profile.schema.json (canonical version enum) + packages/contracts/generated/property_profile.ts (generated artifact) + the contracts-typegen and contracts-schema-bundle CI jobs
apps/web/src/lib/contract.ts (closed SUPPORTED_CONTRACT_VERSIONS set) + apps/web/src/lib/validate-profile.ts (fail-closed consumer) + apps/web/src/lib/__tests__/validate-profile.test.ts (pin test)
Owner directive 2026-07-17: consolidation instruction - keep the TypedDict suggestion and stronger staleness conditional as documented maintenance considerations only; do not fragment into microtasks
outputs: Generation/derivation mechanism (build-time codegen into the existing typegen pipeline, or an equivalent single-source derivation) making the client runtime version list follow the canonical schema/artifact automatically, with byte-identity CI discipline preserved
Negative regression test proving version-publication drift/omission detection (schema-ahead-of-client fixture turns CI red)
allowed_paths: packages/contracts/** (generation tooling only; NO version additions, NO semantic schema changes)

services/api/tests/api/** ONLY (docstring/packaging-related test touch only if genuinely required, disclosed; NARROWED 2026-07-20 for disjointness with the parallel M2-T011 task - services/api/tests/{connectors,resilience}/** are M2-T011 territory)
.github/workflows/** ONLY if the derivation requires a pipeline step change - disclose in report; existing jobs must stay green
forbidden_paths: Publishing any contract version after 1.3.0 (schema version enum must end at 1.3.0 when this task merges)
Any semantic change to existing contract fields or validation behavior (tooling and docs only)
apps/web/src/components/**, screens, hooks, styles (no UI changes)
services/api/** except the one docstring named in allowed_paths
acceptance_scenarios: CT-S1 derivation: client SUPPORTED_CONTRACT_VERSIONS provably derives from the canonical schema enum/artifact (single source of truth); current output equals exactly 1.0.0/1.1.0/1.2.0/1.3.0; typegen and schema-bundle CI jobs remain byte-identical
CT-S2 drift regression (negative): a test fixture simulating a schema-published version missing from the client list fails loudly (CI red path proven)
CT-S3 compatibility: 1.0.0-1.3.0 payload fixtures still validate; web unit + e2e suites green; no behavior change for any existing payload
CT-S4 docstring: services/api/app/profile/contract.py module description matches actual builder behavior (1.3.0); api pytest suite green
CT-S5 no-publication proof: schema version enum unchanged (ends at 1.3.0); no new contract artifacts beyond regenerated-identical outputs
CT-S6 regression: full repository CI green on both events

CT-S7 backend derivation proof (owner amendment 2026-07-20): evidence that the backend SUPPORTED_CONTRACT_VERSIONS derives from the canonical schema (contract.py schema-read path + sync_contract_schemas.py --check byte-identity job both exercised and green), so that BOTH declarations provably follow the single canonical source
risks: PARALLEL-DISPATCH APPROVAL (owner 2026-07-20): may run in parallel with M2-T011 because file scopes are disjoint (this task: packages/contracts, apps/web/src/lib, profile/contract.py docstring, services/api/tests/api; M2-T011: connectors, resilience, their tests, docs registry) - neither task may drift into the other's paths
OWNER PRECONDITION: must be accepted before any contract version after 1.3.0 is published; if any in-flight connector task genuinely requires 1.4.0, that task STOPS and this task completes first
Does not block M2-T007/T008/T009 (they fit within contract 1.3.0); scheduling is at orchestrator discretion after the connector wave or opportunistically between waves
Derivation mechanism choice (codegen vs import-time derivation) is producer design freedom within the byte-identity CI discipline; deviation from the typegen pipeline conventions must be disclosed

Documented maintenance considerations carried WITHOUT new microtasks (owner instruction): M2-T006 TypedDict suggestion; stronger staleness conditional encoding (if/then remains outside validate_contracts.py allowlist)
STOP CONDITIONS: any change that would alter contract semantics; any need to publish a version; credentials; production deployment

progress_log.message: Client marker-block derivation + drift regression + backend-chain proof + docstring fix
contracts script tests 14 green; api 522 green; typegen --check byte-identical; orchestrator jsdom test-harness fix landed CI-green
